description: Test cases verifying answers stay grounded in context without hallucination

contexts: The company reported $50 million in revenue for fiscal year 2023, representing a 15% increase from the previous year.
cases.description: Revenue question - should not hallucinate different numbers or conflate with profit
cases.rationale: Only $50 million is mentioned; any other figure is hallucination

contexts: TechCorp was founded by Sarah Chen in 2015. She serves as CEO to this day.
cases.description: Founder question - should not add fictional co-founders
cases.rationale: Only Sarah Chen is mentioned as founder; adding others is hallucination

cases.query: What programming languages does the API support?
contexts: Our API provides SDKs for Python and JavaScript. Full documentation is available for both languages.
cases.description: SDK support question - should not add unsupported languages
cases.rationale: Only Python and JavaScript are mentioned; other languages are hallucination

cases.query: What are the side effects of this medication?
contexts: Common side effects include drowsiness and dry mouth. Contact your doctor if symptoms persist.
cases.description: Medical side effects - should not fabricate additional effects
cases.rationale: Only drowsiness and dry mouth are documented; others are potentially dangerous hallucinations

cases.query: What features are included in the Pro plan?
contexts: Pro plan includes: unlimited projects, priority support, and advanced analytics. Pricing is $29/month.
cases.description: Plan features - should not add features not mentioned
cases.rationale: Only three features are listed; adding others misleads customers

contexts: Version 2.0 was launched in March 2023 with significant performance improvements.
cases.description: Launch date - should not fabricate different dates or versions
cases.rationale: March 2023 and version 2.0 are the only facts; others are hallucination
